verses.text: To humble Pharaoh, God points to Assyria, once 'a cedar in Lebanon,' towering above the forest. A mighty example. The greatest empires are only trees God planted and can fell.
To humble Pharaoh, God points to Assyria, once 'a cedar in Lebanon,' towering above the forest. A mighty example. The greatest empires are only trees God planted and can fell.
To humble Pharaoh, God points to Assyria, once 'a cedar in Lebanon,' towering above the forest. A mighty example. The greatest empires are only trees God planted and can fell.
The waters made him great; birds nested in his boughs, and 'all great nations' rested in his shade. Wide dominion. His shelter and strength were the envy of the peoples.
The waters made him great; birds nested in his boughs, and 'all great nations' rested in his shade. Wide dominion. His shelter and strength were the envy of the peoples.
The waters made him great; birds nested in his boughs, and 'all great nations' rested in his shade. Wide dominion. His shelter and strength were the envy of the peoples.
He was fairer than every tree, so that even 'the trees... in the garden of God' could not match him. Unrivalled glory. Yet all his beauty was a gift, not his own making.
He was fairer than every tree, so that even 'the trees... in the garden of God' could not match him. Unrivalled glory. Yet all his beauty was a gift, not his own making.
He was fairer than every tree, so that even 'the trees... in the garden of God' could not match him. Unrivalled glory. Yet all his beauty was a gift, not his own making.
Because he lifted himself up in pride, God delivered him to 'the mighty one of the heathen' to be dealt with. Pride brought him down. Height of station only deepens the fall of a haughty heart.
Because he lifted himself up in pride, God delivered him to 'the mighty one of the heathen' to be dealt with. Pride brought him down. Height of station only deepens the fall of a haughty heart.
Strangers cut him down, and his ruin teaches that no tree should exalt itself for its height. A lesson for all. God topples the proud so the watching nations may learn humility.
Strangers cut him down, and his ruin teaches that no tree should exalt itself for its height. A lesson for all. God topples the proud so the watching nations may learn humility.
Strangers cut him down, and his ruin teaches that no tree should exalt itself for its height. A lesson for all. God topples the proud so the watching nations may learn humility.
At his fall God made the nations mourn and tremble, and they went down to the grave with him. Far-reaching ruin. The collapse of the great drags many down in its train.

At his fall God made the nations mourn and tremble, and they went down to the grave with him. Far-reaching ruin. The collapse of the great drags many down in its train.
'To whom art thou thus like in glory?' Yet Pharaoh too shall be brought down to the pit. No exception. The same God who felled Assyria will fell Egypt's proud king.
